sections.paragraph: prop. piercing through, a piercer; hence an executioner; a kind of guards or pretorian soldiers, whose office it was to inflict capital punishments, i.q. כְּרֵתִי. It occurs three times in pl. כָּרִי (for כִּרִים Lehrg. p. 525), 2Ki 11:4, 19 2Ki 11:19, of the attendants of Athaliah; הַכָּרִי וְהָרָצִים executioners and runners, and 2Sa 20:23 in כתיב, of king David’s guards, הַכָּרִי וְהַפְּלֵתִי, הַכְּרֵתִי וגו׳ קרי. [Root כּוּר.]
